module-9-catalog-polymorphism-project.label: Module 9 Catalog Polymorphism Project
module-9-catalog-polymorphism-project.summary: Build a cumulative catalog app with a shared CatalogItem base class, Book and Movie subclasses, a mixed catalog list, and a Catalog class that prints a report.

sketch0.title: Project overview: build one catalog with inheritance and polymorphism

catalog_project_step_1_base_and_book.title: Create CatalogItem and Book
catalog_project_step_1_base_and_book.prompt: Start the module project by building the shared base class. In `models/catalog_item.py`, store `title` in `CatalogItem.__init__` and make `label()` return the title. In `models/book.py`, make `Book` inherit from `CatalogItem`, call `super().__init__(title)`, and store `author`. Do not override `label()` yet. In `main.py`, create `Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")` and print `book.label()`.
catalog_project_step_1_base_and_book.hint: This step should end with a Book object using the inherited base method.

help.concept: Project steps are cumulative: each solution becomes the next starter.
help.hint_1: Begin with the shared title behavior in `CatalogItem`.
help.hint_2: Then connect `Book` to that base class with `super().__init__(title)`.

catalog_project_step_1_base_and_book.starterCode:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")
print(book.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")
print(book.label())

catalog_project_step_1_base_and_book.solutionCode:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")
print(book.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")
print(book.label())

2.message: CatalogItem should store the title attribute.

4.message: Book should accept title and author.

5.message: Book should store title and author.

catalog_project_step_2_add_movie_overrid.title: Add Movie and override label()
catalog_project_step_2_add_movie_overrid.prompt: Continue from the previous solution. Override `Book.label()` so it returns `Book: <title> by <author>`. Then add `models/movie.py` with a `Movie` subclass that stores `minutes` and overrides `label()` to return `Movie: <title> (<minutes> min)`. In `main.py`, create `Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")` and `Movie("Toy Story", 81)`, then print both labels.
catalog_project_step_2_add_movie_overrid.hint: Keep the shared method name `label()` in both subclasses.

help.concept: This step turns inherited behavior into specialized subclass behavior.
help.hint_2: Then give `Movie` its own matching `label()` method.

catalog_project_step_2_add_movie_overrid.starterCode:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")
print(book.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")
print(book.label())

catalog_project_step_2_add_movie_overrid.solutionCode:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie

book = Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")
movie = Movie("Toy Story", 81)
print(book.label())
print(movie.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie

book = Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")
movie = Movie("Toy Story", 81)
print(book.label())
print(movie.label())

0.message: Book.label() should return the specialized book label.

2.message: Movie should accept title and minutes.

3.message: Movie should store title and minutes.

4.message: Movie.label() should return the specialized movie label.

catalog_project_step_3_mixed_catalog_loo.title: Build a mixed catalog list
catalog_project_step_3_mixed_catalog_loo.prompt: Continue from the previous solution. In `main.py`, replace the separate variables with one mixed `catalog` list that contains `Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")` and `Movie("Toy Story", 81)`. Loop through the list and print `item.label()` for each object.
catalog_project_step_3_mixed_catalog_loo.hint: Use one list and one loop instead of separate print calls.

help.concept: This step shows polymorphism directly with a mixed list.
help.hint_2: Loop with `for item in catalog:` and print `item.label()`.

catalog_project_step_3_mixed_catalog_loo.starterCode:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie

book = Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")
movie = Movie("Toy Story", 81)
print(book.label())
print(movie.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie

book = Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien")
movie = Movie("Toy Story", 81)
print(book.label())
print(movie.label())

catalog_project_step_3_mixed_catalog_loo.solutionCode:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie

catalog = [
    Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien"),
    Movie("Toy Story", 81),
]

for item in catalog:
    print(item.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie

catalog = [
    Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien"),
    Movie("Toy Story", 81),
]

for item in catalog:
    print(item.label())

0.message: Create at least one Book object.

1.message: Create at least one Movie object.

2.message: Book.label() should still return the correct label.

3.message: Movie.label() should still return the correct label.

catalog_project_step_4_catalog_class_rep.title: Add the Catalog class report
catalog_project_step_4_catalog_class_rep.prompt: Finish the project by adding `models/catalog.py`. Create a `Catalog` class with `self.items = []`, an `add_item(item)` method that appends to the list, and a `print_report()` method that loops through `self.items` and prints `item.label()`. In `main.py`, create a `Catalog`, add one Book and one Movie, then call `catalog.print_report()`.
catalog_project_step_4_catalog_class_rep.hint: The Catalog class should manage the mixed collection instead of `main.py` doing all the looping.

help.concept: This final step wraps the mixed list inside its own class.
help.hint_2: Use `self.items.append(item)` in `add_item()` and loop through the list in `print_report()`.

catalog_project_step_4_catalog_class_rep.starterCode:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie

catalog = [
    Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien"),
    Movie("Toy Story", 81),
]

for item in catalog:
    print(item.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie

catalog = [
    Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien"),
    Movie("Toy Story", 81),
]

for item in catalog:
    print(item.label())

catalog_project_step_4_catalog_class_rep.solutionCode:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie
from models.catalog import Catalog

catalog = Catalog()
catalog.add_item(Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien"))
catalog.add_item(Movie("Toy Story", 81))
catalog.print_report()

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie
from models.catalog import Catalog

catalog = Catalog()
catalog.add_item(Book("The Hobbit", "J.R.R. Tolkien"))
catalog.add_item(Movie("Toy Story", 81))
catalog.print_report()

2.message: Catalog should store an items list.

module-9-catalog-polymorphism-project-sk.title: Build a small library catalog with inheritance and polymorphism
module-9-catalog-polymorphism-project-sk.bodyMarkdown:
This project ties the whole module together in one domain.

You will:
- create a shared `CatalogItem` base class
- specialize `Book` and `Movie`
- store mixed objects in one collection
- finish with a `Catalog` class that prints a report

Each step should build directly on the previous solution.
